title: ODI Matrix — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing

sections.title: Step 1 — Needs gathered (26 entries)
sections.content:
| Source | Count | Tagging |
|---|---|---|
| Step 06 JTBD (market-level) | 18 | preserves job_step_number 1–7, impact_category, product_related as-is |
| Step 10 product-related | 8 | injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `impact_category="reliability"` (P01/P02 safety override), `product_related=true` |
| **Total** | **26** | — |

No market-level needs dropped; no Step 10 overlap-merged (P01↔N13, P04↔N14, P08↔N11+N12 kept separate per FIT file ruling that solution-agnostic market need + causal-mechanism product need are distinct entries).

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction recalibration (incumbent-anchored)
sections.content:
Incumbent reference set per Step 03 / JTBD Phase 2: **Piller UNIBLOCK + Hitec Power (flywheel, S1 dominant)**, Schneider Galaxy VXL / Eaton / Vertiv (static UPS, tool-level), ABB PCS100 AVC + AMSC D-VAR (DVR sub-cycle), Fluence / Sungrow / Wärtsilä (emerging BESS, A4 slot).

### JTBD recalibrations (Step 06 needs only)

| ID | S_orig | S_recal | Evidence driving shift |
|---|---|---|---|
| N08 | 4 | **6** | Piller / Hitec flywheel deliver sub-50 ms ride-through with SEMI F47 track record at S1 fabs — incumbent set *does* satisfy the sub-200 ms proof in practice |
| N10 | 4 | **5** | Piller-protected fabs show documented wafer-scrap avoidance at sag events — raise S marginally for incumbent average |
| N12 | 4 | **6** | Rotating-mass flywheel source transition is intrinsically <16 ms; DVR sub-cycle injection also satisfies — incumbent set strong |
| N13 | 5 | **6** | Flywheel (A1) and DVR (A3) carry zero high-energy-density fire risk; incumbent set on this market broadly satisfies (only A4 entrants drag S down) |
| all others | — | **unchanged** | no Step 03 evidence contradicts original LLM estimate |

### Step 10 product-related recalibrations

| ID | S_orig | S_recal | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| P01 | 5 | **4** | Moss Landing 2024 + McMicken thermal-runaway precedents suggest S was optimistic even across Fluence / Sungrow / Tesla containers in fab-adjacent settings |
| P03 | 4 | **5** | Fluence / Wärtsilä ship RTE performance guarantees (AC-AC ≥85%) — modest satisfaction lift |
| all others | — | **unchanged** | structural Li-ion chemistry issues (supply chain CN concentration, €/kWh band, m²/MWh density, dispatch-SoC vs sub-200 ms) unresolved by any A4 incumbent |

Importance scores unchanged across all 26 needs (importance-stability rule — the jobs are inherent to the fab context).

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity scores (deterministic)
sections.content:
Formula: `opp = I + max(I − S, 0)`  ·  `is_underserved = I>7 AND S<5`  ·  `is_overserved = I<3 AND S>7`.

### JTBD entries (18)

| ID | Step | Statement (short) | I | S | Opp | Under | Over |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N05 | PREPARE | Cert delay pushing COD >60 d | 10 | 3 | **17.00** | ✅ | — |
| N11 | EXECUTE | Revenue-stack dispatch vs ride-through window | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | ✅ | — |
| N01 | DEFINE | Under-spec sag envelope → wafer loss | 10 | 5 | **15.00** | — | — |
| N10 | EXECUTE | Tool-state scrap >100 wafers/event | 10 | 5 | **15.00** | — | — |
| N03 | LOCATE | MV feeder capacity shortfall | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | ✅ | — |
| N06 | PREPARE | Parallel cert >6 mo critical path | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | ✅ | — |
| N08 | CONFIRM | Sub-200 ms ITIC ride-through proof | 10 | 6 | **14.00** | — | — |
| N12 | EXECUTE | ≤16 ms source-transition | 10 | 6 | **14.00** | — | — |
| N13 | EXECUTE | Fire event → >24 h evacuation | 10 | 6 | **14.00** | — | — |
| N18 | EXECUTE | Netzentgelte + decarb board scorecard | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | ✅ | — |
| N14 | MONITOR | Autonomy degradation detection >3%/yr | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | ✅ | — |
| N02 | DEFINE | Revenue-stack vs ride-through objective conflict | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | — | — |
| N09 | CONFIRM | DCS handshake ≤5 discrepancies | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | — | — |
| N15 | MONITOR | Discriminate real disturbances from false trips | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | — | — |
| N17 | EXECUTE | Audit-ready power-event logs (P3) | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | — | — |
| N04 | LOCATE | Fire-clearance zoning layout iterations | 6 | 3 | **9.00** | — | — |
| N07 | PREPARE | Commissioning-stage energy-stack defects | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | — | — |
| N16 | MODIFY | Firmware updates >2 h downtime | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | — | — |

### Step 10 product-related entries (8)

| ID | Statement (short) | I | S | Opp | Under | Over |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P08 | Dispatch SoC vs ≤16 ms transition | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | ✅ | — |
| P06 | €/kWh >400 → CFO capex rejection | 9 | 3 | **15.00** | ✅ | — |
| P01 | Li-ion thermal runaway → >24 h evacuation | 10 | 4 | **16.00** | ✅ | — |
| P05 | LFP cell supply disruption (CN / EU Batt Reg) | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | ✅ | — |
| P02 | Fire-clearance setback >15 m → siting outside perimeter | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | ✅ | — |
| P03 | 10–15% RTE loss eroding revenue stack | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | — | — |
| P04 | HVAC duty-cycling → autonomy erosion >5%/24 mo | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | — | — |
| P07 | Container footprint + fire-access >20% yard area | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | — | — |

**Sum of opp scores:** JTBD = 222.00 · Step 10 = 101.00 · **Total = 323.00** · **Average = 12.42**

**Underserved (I>7 AND S<5):** N03, N05, N06, N11, N14, N18, P01, P02, P05, P06, P08 = **11 entries**
**Overserved (I<3 AND S>7):** **0 entries** (adoption is emerging; no incumbent comfortably satisfies fab-grade BESS needs)

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-check vs Feature-Market-Fit (Step 09)
sections.content:
FIT Step 09 avg overall_fit across 6 technology features = **4.69** ( (3.56+5.22+4.11+5.67+6.33+3.22) / 6 ).
ODI avg opp score = **12.42**.

| Threshold | Result |
|---|---|
| ODI avg > 12 AND FIT avg < 4 → "high opp / low fit" | **Not triggered** (FIT 4.69 > 4) |
| ODI avg < 8 AND FIT avg > 7 → "low opp / high fit" | **Not triggered** |
| Narrative read | High opportunity market (12.42) is **directionally aligned** with moderate-but-positive feature fit (4.69). Top ODI drivers (N05 cert, N11/P08 dispatch-vs-transition, P01 fire) map cleanly onto the two strongest features F5 Grid-Code Cert Delivery (6.33) and F4 EMS (5.67). Dealkiller pattern from FIT (P01, P08, P06) matches 3 of the top-5 ODI entries → internally consistent. |

No cross-check warnings emitted.

---

sections.title: Validation (QA checklist)
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula `opp = I + max(I−S, 0)` — spot-check N05 (10+7=17 ✓), P08 (9+7=16 ✓), N13 (10+4=14 ✓), P03 (7+2=9 ✓), N16 (6+1=7 ✓) | **PASS** |
| Q2 | `is_underserved = I>7 AND S<5` — 11 entries flagged match predicate exhaustively (N03, N05, N06, N11, N14, N18, P01, P02, P05, P06, P08) | **PASS** |
| Q3 | `is_overserved = I<3 AND S>7` — 0 entries, 0 predicate-positives | **PASS** |
| Q4 | Need count preserved: JTBD contributed 18 (Step 06), Step 10 added 8 → 26 ≥ 18 | **PASS** |
| Q5 | Top-5 sorted by descending opp score | **PASS** |
| Q6 | Cross-check vs Step 09 ran → no-inconsistency note emitted | **PASS** |

---

sections.title: Strategic Read
sections.content:
| Insight | Evidence |
|---|---|
| **Certification competence is the #1 monetisable opportunity** | N05 opp=17 alone tops the matrix; N06 parallel-cert opp=14; N08 SEMI F47 proof opp=14 → clusters onto FIT F5 (6.33) |
| **Dispatch-vs-transition is the structural Li-ion conflict** | N11 + N12 + P08 all at opp ≥14; P08 at S=2 is the matrix's worst-served need — engineering must solve this before fab reference |
| **Fire posture is the permit-gate dealkiller** | N13 (14) + P01 (16) + P02 (12) stack on fire-clearance / thermal-runaway → engineered suppression envelope + setback non-negotiable |
| **Net-Zero reporting is the CFO hook** | N18 opp=13 underserved; only ODI entry that maps to FIT F4 EMS (5.67) where Piller / Eaton deliver nothing |
| **Supply-chain and CAPEX are entry gates** | P05 (13) + P06 (15) both underserved — must be hedged via KfW / innovation tenders and dual-source LFP before bid submission |

Consistent with JTBD + FIT strategic read: **MEDIUM priority — defer direct fab pursuit** until reference installations land in HIGH-priority markets. Enter S4 greenfield + S5 retrofit as subcontractor to fab GC (M+W, Exyte, Jacobs), leading with PV+BESS decarbonisation scope (N18) while ride-through qualification (N08, N10, N11, N12) matures.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
Upstream files only (no web search):
- `01_product_decomposition.md` — features F1–F6, constraints, commodity FP
- `02_functional_promise.md` — commodity-level FP anchor
- `05_market_discovery.md` — market identity (NAICS 334413, distance 6, MEDIUM priority)
- `JTBD_semiconductor_and_related_device_manufac.md` — N01–N18 error statements, incumbent anchors (Piller, Hitec, Schneider, Eaton, ABB, Fluence, Sungrow, Wärtsilä)
- `FIT_semiconductor_and_related_device_manufac.md` — Step 09 feature-fit scores, Step 10 product-related needs P01–P08

rows.Evidence driving shift: Piller / Hitec flywheel deliver sub-50 ms ride-through with SEMI F47 track record at S1 fabs — incumbent set *does* satisfy the sub-200 ms proof in practice
Piller-protected fabs show documented wafer-scrap avoidance at sag events — raise S marginally for incumbent average
Rotating-mass flywheel source transition is intrinsically <16 ms; DVR sub-cycle injection also satisfies — incumbent set strong
Flywheel (A1) and DVR (A3) carry zero high-energy-density fire risk; incumbent set on this market broadly satisfies (only A4 entrants drag S down)
no Step 03 evidence contradicts original LLM estimate

rows.Evidence: Moss Landing 2024 + McMicken thermal-runaway precedents suggest S was optimistic even across Fluence / Sungrow / Tesla containers in fab-adjacent settings
Fluence / Wärtsilä ship RTE performance guarantees (AC-AC ≥85%) — modest satisfaction lift
structural Li-ion chemistry issues (supply chain CN concentration, €/kWh band, m²/MWh density, dispatch-SoC vs sub-200 ms) unresolved by any A4 incumbent

rows.Statement (short): Discriminate real disturbances from false trips
LFP cell supply disruption (CN / EU Batt Reg)
Fire-clearance setback >15 m → siting outside perimeter

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of certification delay pushing fab COD beyond planned ramp date by >60 days (N05)
Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state compromising the sub-200 ms ride-through window by >1 event per quarter (N11)
Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state of charge compromising the ≤16 ms source-transition window during a grid disturbance (P08)
Minimize the likelihood of a high-energy-density Li-ion thermal runaway event triggering >24 h fab-wide evacuation (P01)
Minimize the likelihood of under-specifying the voltage-sag envelope such that >5% of fab tools lose wafer batches per SEMI F47 event (N01)

rows.Threshold: ODI avg > 12 AND FIT avg < 4 → "high opp / low fit"
ODI avg < 8 AND FIT avg > 7 → "low opp / high fit"

rows.Result: High opportunity market (12.42) is **directionally aligned** with moderate-but-positive feature fit (4.69). Top ODI drivers (N05 cert, N11/P08 dispatch-vs-transition, P01 fire) map cleanly onto the two strongest features F5 Grid-Code Cert Delivery (6.33) and F4 EMS (5.67). Dealkiller pattern from FIT (P01, P08, P06) matches 3 of the top-5 ODI entries → internally consistent.

rows.Check: `is_underserved = I>7 AND S<5` — 11 entries flagged match predicate exhaustively (N03, N05, N06, N11, N14, N18, P01, P02, P05, P06, P08)
Need count preserved: JTBD contributed 18 (Step 06), Step 10 added 8 → 26 ≥ 18
Top-5 sorted by descending opp score
Cross-check vs Step 09 ran → no-inconsistency note emitted

rows.Evidence: N05 opp=17 alone tops the matrix; N06 parallel-cert opp=14; N08 SEMI F47 proof opp=14 → clusters onto FIT F5 (6.33)
N11 + N12 + P08 all at opp ≥14; P08 at S=2 is the matrix's worst-served need — engineering must solve this before fab reference
N13 (14) + P01 (16) + P02 (12) stack on fire-clearance / thermal-runaway → engineered suppression envelope + setback non-negotiable
N18 opp=13 underserved; only ODI entry that maps to FIT F4 EMS (5.67) where Piller / Eaton deliver nothing
P05 (13) + P06 (15) both underserved — must be hedged via KfW / innovation tenders and dual-source LFP before bid submission

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of under-specifying the voltage-sag envelope such that >5% of fab tools lose wafer batches per SEMI F47 event
Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack objective conflicting with ride-through readiness, eroding >15% of projected annual dispatch revenue
Minimize the likelihood of MV feeder capacity shortfall blocking fab load-growth by >10% of planned ramp capacity
Minimize the likelihood of fire-clearance zoning requiring >2 site-layout iterations post-permit submission
Minimize the likelihood of certification delay pushing fab COD beyond planned ramp date by >60 days
Minimize the likelihood of parallel plant-connection and process-equipment certifications adding >6 months to critical path
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning-stage energy-stack defects causing >3 module swaps before handover
Increase the ability to demonstrate sub-200 ms ride-through on the ITIC curve with documented ≥99.9% event coverage at site acceptance
entries.recalibration_note: S raised 4→6; Piller/Hitec flywheel incumbents deliver sub-50ms in S1 fabs

entries.error_statement: Increase the ability to complete fab-DCS handshake testing with ≤5 SEMI-protocol discrepancies before commissioning release
Minimize the likelihood of unrecovered tool state from a voltage disturbance causing >100 wafers of lot scrap per event
entries.recalibration_note: S raised 4→5; Piller-protected fabs show documented scrap avoidance

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state compromising the sub-200 ms ride-through window by >1 event per quarter

entries.error_statement: Increase the ability to detect autonomy degradation >3%/year before contracted ride-through duration is breached

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of fire-clearance zoning adding >15 m setback that forces BESS siting outside the fab security perimeter

entries.recalibration_note: S raised 4→5; Fluence/Wärtsilä AC-AC ≥85% RTE guarantees

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of ambient-extreme HVAC duty cycling reducing contracted ride-through autonomy by >5% within 24 months
Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell supply disruption (CN concentration, EU Battery Reg compliance) adding >12 weeks to fab-ramp-critical COD
Minimize the likelihood of installed cost exceeding €400/kWh such that CFO capex board rejects the project despite revenue-stack model

Minimize the likelihood of revenue-stack dispatch state of charge compromising the ≤16 ms source-transition window during a grid disturbance
